title: I Built a Private Genomics Study with Stoffel MPC
excerpt: Most genomic studies begin by asking participants to upload one of the most identifying and irrevocable pieces of data they own, their DNA. I continue to investigate whether multi-party computation…
content_html:
Most genomic studies begin by asking participants to upload one of the most identifying and irrevocable pieces of data they own, their DNA. I continue to investigate whether multi-party computation can offer a different model, one in which a study can produce useful results without anyone collecting the participants’ genomes in the first place.

That question has now led me to build a proof of concept with Stoffel MPC. One hundred simulated participants successfully computed aggregate allele counts without any party seeing the complete dataset. This post explains what I built, how it performed and what I learned.

Stoffel MPC has launched

Stoffel recently launched version 0.1.0 of its multi-party computation (MPC) platform with the ambitious goal of making privacy-preserving applications accessible to ordinary developers and not just to teams of cryptography boffins.

MPC lets several computers perform calculations on private data without any one of the computers receiving the complete inputs. Although MPC systems have traditionally been difficult to build with Stoffel presents the technology as an approachable developer platform. Applications are written in its Python-like StoffelLang, compiled to bytecode and executed by the Stoffel VM across a set of MPC parties.

We no longer have to rely on a more robust privacy policy or a more secure central database. We can simply avoid collecting the raw data in one place.

Naturally, I wanted to try it with Monadic DNA

Given my ongoing work on Monadic DNA, genomics was an obvious test case. In an earlier experiment, my colleagues and I used Nillion and real genotype data from thirty participants to explore private DNA analysis.

Genetic data is an unusually good test of a privacy system. It is highly identifying, can reveal information about relatives and cannot be changed after a breach. At the same time, many useful genomic studies do not need to inspect individual records. A researcher may only need cohort-level variant counts or a score calculated across the group.

The concrete use case I wanted to explore was a revamped Monadic DNA mobile app. A Monadic DNA user keeps their genotype data on their phone and may choose to participate in a study that needs aggregate statistics across many users (perhaps in return for a payment). The question was whether the app could contribute that data securely without first uploading the user’s complete genotype to Monadic DNA, the researcher or another trusted central service.

Instead of the conventional design where everyone’s genotype data is uploaded to a central service that must protect it, I wanted to see whether Stoffel could let each Monadic DNA app contribute directly to a joint calculation. In that design, no coordinator, application server or individual MPC party receives the complete dataset.

The source code for the experiment is available at github.com/vishakh/stoffel-test.

What the proof of concept is meant to do

The proof of concept simulates one hundred Monadic DNA mobile-app users joining a genomic aggregate study. Each user contributes six synthetic SNP values, which they could have obtained through Monadic DNA, 23andMe or another service. The study calculates the total count for each target allele and a simple weighted score across the cohort.

The important part is the trust boundary. Each simulated user has a separate client identity and a process containing only that user’s six values. The client creates shares locally and sends a different share directly to each MPC party. Only the aggregate counts and score are revealed.

This is deliberately different from putting all one hundred records into one server-side client. That would demonstrate private arithmetic inside the MPC network, but the client would remain a trusted data collector capable of seeing or leaking every genotype.

In the intended Monadic DNA flow, a user would review and consent to a study in the mobile app. The app would select only the DNA values required for that approved computation, create the shares on the phone and send them directly to the MPC parties. Complete DNA data should never pass through Monadic DNA’s servers or another central ingestion service; only the approved aggregate result should leave the MPC computation.

Comparing MPC and fully homomorphic encryption

Multi-party computation (MPC) and fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) both make it possible to calculate over data without exposing the underlying inputs, but they use different models. With FHE, each user typically encrypts their genotype under a public key and a server computes directly on the ciphertexts. With MPC, each user divides their input into shares and sends a different share to each of several computing parties.

The approaches place trust and operational complexity in different places. An FHE system needs a policy for who can decrypt the result. This may involve one key holder or a threshold key shared across several parties. MPC does not rely on one decryption key holder but it requires several sufficiently independent parties to communicate and remain available during the computation.

The performance characteristics also differ by workload and implementation. FHE supports non-interactive encrypted inputs and computation by a single service, although ciphertext operations can be computationally expensive. MPC introduces communication between parties, while simple aggregate arithmetic such as the sums in this experiment is a natural MPC workload. Neither approach is universally better for genomics. The appropriate choice depends on the computation, trust assumptions, deployment constraints and desired interaction model. This PoC explores the MPC option through Stoffel rather than attempting to establish a general advantage over FHE.

To learn more about using FHE for building consumer applications, see https://vishakh.blog/2025/08/06/lessons-from-using-fhe-to-build-a-secure-consumer-app/.

In August 2026, I will also be giving a talk about FHE at the HOPE 26 conference: https://schedule.hope.net/hope26/talks#Using-Fully-Homomorphic-Encryption-to-Build-Real-World-Software

Technical details

The local setup has three types of components:

  • One hundred native client processes represent independent users or mobile devices. Each receives a unique, short-lived identity and only its own six synthetic SNP values.
  • Four Stoffel MPC parties receive shares from every client and jointly execute the private computation.
  • A coordinator authorizes the participants and handles discovery. It does not receive genotype values or perform the genomic calculation.

The coordinator and four MPC parties run in Docker. The clients run as independent native processes outside the Docker network and connect through exposed local addresses. This demonstrates that user-side clients do not inherently need to run alongside the MPC infrastructure.

The private computation is written in StoffelLang. Values marked secret remain shared while the program operates on them. The core aggregation function reads one private value from each user’s input slot and adds the values together:

The program repeats this for all six SNPs. It reveals only the cohort totals and then calculates the weighted cohort score. It never deliberately reveals an individual contribution.

The four parties use Stoffel’s HoneyBadger backend over the BLS12-381 scalar field. In developer terms, HoneyBadger is Stoffel’s general-purpose engine for private arithmetic. The configuration uses four parties and a threshold of one, satisfying HoneyBadger’s n >= 3t + 1 requirement and tolerating one faulty party under the protocol’s assumptions so one bad apple cannot ruin the basket of MPC nodes.

The runner provisions identities, compiles the program, starts the infrastructure and launches clients 50 milliseconds apart. It independently calculates the expected result for the deterministic synthetic fixture, checks the aggregate emitted by the MPC parties and records timing and memory usage. Everything is torn down after the run.

Findings

The one-hundred-user run completed successfully and produced the expected values:

The weighted cohort score was 498, matching the result calculated independently from the fixture.

On my development PC, the benchmark measured:

  • 28.26 seconds to start and prepare the infrastructure;
  • 6.17 seconds to launch the clients and complete the MPC workload;
  • 45.29 seconds for the complete run, including setup, verification and cleanup;
  • approximately 1.29 GiB peak memory across the one hundred client processes; and
  • approximately 62 MiB peak memory across the coordinator and four MPC containers.

Most of the elapsed time was infrastructure startup rather than the private calculation. This is a local proof of concept rather than a production benchmark but an end-to-end run in under a minute was encouraging.

Scaling from ten to one hundred clients also produced a useful infrastructure lesson. Every client connects to the coordinator and all four parties, creating roughly five hundred client-to-server connections. Launching all clients simultaneously caused intermittent TLS failures while clients waited for MPC input masks. The cryptographic computation was not the bottleneck; the issue was the initial burst of network connections.

Staggering client launches by just 50 milliseconds spread the handshake burst over about five seconds and produced a successful run. It was an encouragingly small adjustment, and a useful reminder that an MPC application is more than its cryptographic protocol. Identity provisioning, TLS handshakes, connection limits, retries, backoff and observability all matter as participation grows.

Developer experience

So does Stoffel make MPC-ing easy? The core developer experience was one of the most encouraging parts of the experiment. StoffelLang is small and readable, its secret types make the privacy boundary visible in the program, and writing the six private sums felt much closer to ordinary application development than to implementing a cryptographic protocol. The repository examples provided a useful path to getting a local coordinator and MPC parties running.

For a version 0.1.0 release, the documentation and examples gave me enough to move from a small example to a working 100-client experiment. The straightforward single-client path is explained clearly. This PoC went beyond that path, using many separately identified clients outside Docker and tuning input-mask capacity and output authorization, so some answers came from the examples and source code rather than a single guide. The code was readable enough to make that investigation productive.

The fact that the private computation was the easiest part is a good sign for Stoffel’s developer-platform goal. As the platform matures, end-to-end deployment guides, a configuration reference, troubleshooting documentation and supported client SDKs could make the same experience even smoother for developers who do not want to inspect implementation details.

The test also clarified the next steps from a successful PoC toward production:

  • All four MPC parties ran on one PC. The protocol separated their data, but one machine is not four independent organizations. A production deployment needs parties operated by entities that are unlikely to collude. With this configuration, privacy assumes no more than one party is faulty or colluding. Happily, the Stoffel team provided me with a API key to their MPC cluster so I can test a near-production deployment soon.
  • The inputs were deterministic and synthetic. The test harness knew them so it could verify the answer. In a real study, independently operated devices must generate shares locally.
  • MPC protects the computation, not the safety of the result. Small cohorts, repeated queries or overlapping groups can reveal individual contributions by subtraction. Production still needs minimum cohort sizes, query controls, consent, auditing and a separate identity authorized to receive results.
  • This repository does not yet provide a production-ready mobile SDK. The native Rust clients stand in for phones; packaging and auditing that logic for iOS and Android remains necessary. MPC using hundreds of mobile devices is still pretty niche so the lack of a mobile SDK is far from unreasonable.
  • The successful launch staggering is appropriate for this PoC. Testing one thousand simultaneous clients would be the next scale milestone and would require higher RPC limits, robust retries, more memory and realistic infrastructure.

Conclusion

Stoffel made the private computation itself pleasantly straightforward. StoffelLang was expressive enough for the aggregate, the four-party network produced the correct result and one hundred independently identified clients completed the study on a single development machine.

The remaining questions sit around the computation, i.e. who operates the parties, how identities are issued, what happens when mobile clients disconnect and which aggregates are safe to reveal. Stoffel allowed the experiment to move the trust boundary away from a central genotype database and into explicit product and infrastructure decisions that can be designed and audited.

There is naturally more work between a version 0.1.0 PoC and a production deployment. The immediate next step is to run the program on a real cluster operated by the Stoffel team, moving beyond the single-machine local setup (coming soon!). Beyond that, useful steps include independently operated parties, audited mobile client code, resilient connection handling and larger load tests. Still, Stoffel successfully demonstrated the most important part: a credible path to genomic studies that do not begin by collecting everyone’s raw DNA in one database.

For an initial release and a local proof of concept, that is an impressive result. It leaves me cautiously optimistic about Stoffel and genuinely interested in building further with it as the platform develops.

blocks.html_en: Instead of the conventional design where everyone’s genotype data is uploaded to a central service that must protect it, I wanted to see whether Stoffel could let each Monadic DNA app contribute directly to a joint calculation. In that design, no coordinator, application server or individual MPC party receives the complete dataset.

sentences.en: Instead of the conventional design where everyone’s genotype data is uploaded to a central service that must protect it, I wanted to see whether Stoffel could let each Monadic DNA app contribute directly to a joint calculation.
sentences.zh: 我想看看 Stoffel 是否可以让每个 Monadic DNA 应用程序直接参与联合计算，而不是采用将每个人的基因型数据上传到必须保护它的中央服务的传统设计。

sentences.en: In that design, no coordinator, application server or individual MPC party receives the complete dataset.
sentences.zh: 在该设计中，协调器、应用程序服务器或单个 MPC 方不会接收完整的数据集。

blocks.html_en: The source code for the experiment is available at github.com/vishakh/stoffel-test.

sentences.en: The source code for the experiment is available at github.com/vishakh/stoffel-test.
sentences.zh: 该实验的源代码可在 github.com/vishakh/stoffel-test 获取。

blocks.html_en: What the proof of concept is meant to do

sentences.en: What the proof of concept is meant to do
sentences.zh: 概念验证的目的是什么

blocks.html_en: The proof of concept simulates one hundred Monadic DNA mobile-app users joining a genomic aggregate study. Each user contributes six synthetic SNP values, which they could have obtained through Monadic DNA, 23andMe or another service. The study calculates the total count for each target allele and a simple weighted score across the cohort.

sentences.en: The proof of concept simulates one hundred Monadic DNA mobile-app users joining a genomic aggregate study.
sentences.zh: 概念验证模拟了一百名 Monadic DNA 移动应用用户参与基因组聚合研究。

sentences.en: Each user contributes six synthetic SNP values, which they could have obtained through Monadic DNA, 23andMe or another service.
sentences.zh: 每个用户贡献六个合成 SNP 值，他们可以通过 Monadic DNA、23andMe 或其他服务获得这些值。

sentences.en: The study calculates the total count for each target allele and a simple weighted score across the cohort.
sentences.zh: 该研究计算了每个目标等位基因的总数以及整个队列的简单加权得分。

blocks.html_en: The important part is the trust boundary. Each simulated user has a separate client identity and a process containing only that user’s six values. The client creates shares locally and sends a different share directly to each MPC party. Only the aggregate counts and score are revealed.

sentences.en: The important part is the trust boundary.
sentences.zh: 重要的部分是信任边界。

sentences.en: Each simulated user has a separate client identity and a process containing only that user’s six values.
sentences.zh: 每个模拟用户都有一个单独的客户端身份和一个仅包含该用户的六个值的进程。

sentences.en: The client creates shares locally and sends a different share directly to each MPC party.
sentences.zh: 客户端在本地创建共享并将不同的共享直接发送到每个 MPC 方。

sentences.en: Only the aggregate counts and score are revealed.
sentences.zh: 仅显示总计数和分数。

blocks.html_en: This is deliberately different from putting all one hundred records into one server-side client. That would demonstrate private arithmetic inside the MPC network, but the client would remain a trusted data collector capable of seeing or leaking every genotype.

sentences.en: This is deliberately different from putting all one hundred records into one server-side client.
sentences.zh: 这与将所有一百条记录放入一个服务器端客户端是故意不同的。

sentences.en: That would demonstrate private arithmetic inside the MPC network, but the client would remain a trusted data collector capable of seeing or leaking every genotype.
sentences.zh: 这将展示 MPC 网络内部的私有算术，但客户端仍将是一个可信的数据收集器，能够查看或泄露每个基因型。

blocks.html_en: In the intended Monadic DNA flow, a user would review and consent to a study in the mobile app. The app would select only the DNA values required for that approved computation, create the shares on the phone and send them directly to the MPC parties. Complete DNA data should never pass through Monadic DNA’s servers or another central ingestion service; only the approved aggregate result should leave the MPC computation.

sentences.en: In the intended Monadic DNA flow, a user would review and consent to a study in the mobile app.
sentences.zh: 在预期的 Monadic DNA 流程中，用户将在移动应用程序中查看并同意一项研究。

sentences.en: The app would select only the DNA values required for that approved computation, create the shares on the phone and send them directly to the MPC parties.
sentences.zh: 该应用程序将仅选择经批准的计算所需的 DNA 值，在手机上创建共享并将其直接发送给 MPC 各方。

sentences.en: Complete DNA data should never pass through Monadic DNA’s servers or another central ingestion service; only the approved aggregate result should leave the MPC computation.
sentences.zh: 完整的 DNA 数据不应通过 Monadic DNA 的服务器或其他中央摄取服务；只有批准的汇总结果才应离开 MPC 计算。

blocks.html_en: Comparing MPC and fully homomorphic encryption

sentences.en: Comparing MPC and fully homomorphic encryption
sentences.zh: MPC 和全同态加密的比较

blocks.html_en: Multi-party computation (MPC) and fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) both make it possible to calculate over data without exposing the underlying inputs, but they use different models. With FHE, each user typically encrypts their genotype under a public key and a server computes directly on the ciphertexts. With MPC, each user divides their input into shares and sends a different share to each of several computing parties.

sentences.en: Multi-party computation (MPC) and fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) both make it possible to calculate over data without exposing the underlying inputs, but they use different models.
sentences.zh: 多方计算 (MPC) 和完全同态加密 (FHE) 都可以在不暴露底层输入的情况下计算数据，但它们使用不同的模型。

sentences.en: With FHE, each user typically encrypts their genotype under a public key and a server computes directly on the ciphertexts.
sentences.zh: 使用 FHE，每个用户通常使用公钥对其基因型进行加密，服务器直接对密文进行计算。

sentences.en: With MPC, each user divides their input into shares and sends a different share to each of several computing parties.
sentences.zh: 通过 MPC，每个用户将其输入分成多个份额，并将不同的份额发送给多个计算方中的每一个。

blocks.html_en: The approaches place trust and operational complexity in different places. An FHE system needs a policy for who can decrypt the result. This may involve one key holder or a threshold key shared across several parties. MPC does not rely on one decryption key holder but it requires several sufficiently independent parties to communicate and remain available during the computation.

sentences.en: The approaches place trust and operational complexity in different places.
sentences.zh: 这些方法将信任和操作复杂性放在不同的地方。

sentences.en: An FHE system needs a policy for who can decrypt the result.
sentences.zh: FHE 系统需要制定谁可以解密结果的策略。

sentences.en: This may involve one key holder or a threshold key shared across several parties.
sentences.zh: 这可能涉及一个密钥持有者或多方共享的阈值密钥。

sentences.en: MPC does not rely on one decryption key holder but it requires several sufficiently independent parties to communicate and remain available during the computation.
sentences.zh: MPC 不依赖于一个解密密钥持有者，但它需要多个足够独立的参与方进行通信并在计算过程中保持可用。

blocks.html_en: The performance characteristics also differ by workload and implementation. FHE supports non-interactive encrypted inputs and computation by a single service, although ciphertext operations can be computationally expensive. MPC introduces communication between parties, while simple aggregate arithmetic such as the sums in this experiment is a natural MPC workload. Neither approach is universally better for genomics. The appropriate choice depends on the computation, trust assumptions, deployment constraints and desired interaction model. This PoC explores the MPC option through Stoffel rather than attempting to establish a general advantage over FHE.

sentences.en: The performance characteristics also differ by workload and implementation.
sentences.zh: 性能特征还因工作负载和实施而异。

sentences.en: FHE supports non-interactive encrypted inputs and computation by a single service, although ciphertext operations can be computationally expensive.
sentences.zh: FHE 支持单个服务的非交互式加密输入和计算，尽管密文操作的计算成本可能很高。

sentences.en: MPC introduces communication between parties, while simple aggregate arithmetic such as the sums in this experiment is a natural MPC workload.
sentences.zh: MPC 引入了各方之间的通信，而简单的聚合算术（例如本实验中的求和）是自然的 MPC 工作负载。

sentences.en: Neither approach is universally better for genomics.
sentences.zh: 这两种方法对于基因组学来说都不是更好。

sentences.en: The appropriate choice depends on the computation, trust assumptions, deployment constraints and desired interaction model.
sentences.zh: 适当的选择取决于计算、信任假设、部署约束和所需的交互模型。

sentences.en: This PoC explores the MPC option through Stoffel rather than attempting to establish a general advantage over FHE.
sentences.zh: 此 PoC 通过 Stoffel 探索 MPC 选项，而不是试图建立相对于 FHE 的总体优势。

blocks.html_en: To learn more about using FHE for building consumer applications, see https://vishakh.blog/2025/08/06/lessons-from-using-fhe-to-build-a-secure-consumer-app/.

sentences.en: To learn more about using FHE for building consumer applications, see https://vishakh.blog/2025/08/06/lessons-from-using-fhe-to-build-a-secure-consumer-app/.
sentences.zh: 要了解有关使用 FHE 构建消费者应用程序的更多信息，请参阅 https://vishakh.blog/2025/08/06/lessons-from-using-fhe-to-build-a-secure-consumer-app/。

blocks.html_en: In August 2026, I will also be giving a talk about FHE at the HOPE 26 conference: https://schedule.hope.net/hope26/talks#Using-Fully-Homomorphic-Encryption-to-Build-Real-World-Software

sentences.en: In August 2026, I will also be giving a talk about FHE at the HOPE 26 conference: https://schedule.hope.net/hope26/talks#Using-Fully-Homomorphic-Encryption-to-Build-Real-World-Software
sentences.zh: 2026 年 8 月，我还将在 HOPE 26 会议上发表有关 FHE 的演讲：https://schedule.hope.net/hope26/talks#Using-Fully-Homomorphic-Encryption-to-Build-Real-World-Software

blocks.html_en: Technical details

sentences.en: Technical details
sentences.zh: 技术细节

blocks.html_en: The local setup has three types of components:

sentences.en: The local setup has three types of components:
sentences.zh: 本地设置具有三种类型的组件：

blocks.html_en: • One hundred native client processes represent independent users or mobile devices. Each receives a unique, short-lived identity and only its own six synthetic SNP values.

sentences.en: One hundred native client processes represent independent users or mobile devices.
sentences.zh: 一百个本机客户端进程代表独立用户或移动设备。

sentences.en: Each receives a unique, short-lived identity and only its own six synthetic SNP values.
sentences.zh: 每个都有一个独特的、短暂的身份，并且只有自己的六个合成 SNP 值。

blocks.html_en: • Four Stoffel MPC parties receive shares from every client and jointly execute the private computation.

sentences.en: Four Stoffel MPC parties receive shares from every client and jointly execute the private computation.
sentences.zh: 四个 Stoffel MPC 参与方接收每个客户的份额并共同执行私密计算。

blocks.html_en: • A coordinator authorizes the participants and handles discovery. It does not receive genotype values or perform the genomic calculation.

sentences.en: A coordinator authorizes the participants and handles discovery.
sentences.zh: 协调员授权参与者并处理发现。

sentences.en: It does not receive genotype values or perform the genomic calculation.
sentences.zh: 它不接收基因型值或执行基因组计算。

blocks.html_en: The coordinator and four MPC parties run in Docker. The clients run as independent native processes outside the Docker network and connect through exposed local addresses. This demonstrates that user-side clients do not inherently need to run alongside the MPC infrastructure.

sentences.en: The coordinator and four MPC parties run in Docker.
sentences.zh: 协调者和四个 MPC 方在 Docker 中运行。

sentences.en: The clients run as independent native processes outside the Docker network and connect through exposed local addresses.
sentences.zh: 客户端作为独立的本机进程在 Docker 网络外部运行，并通过公开的本地地址进行连接。

sentences.en: This demonstrates that user-side clients do not inherently need to run alongside the MPC infrastructure.
sentences.zh: 这表明用户端客户端本质上不需要与 MPC 基础设施一起运行。

blocks.html_en: The private computation is written in StoffelLang. Values marked secret remain shared while the program operates on them. The core aggregation function reads one private value from each user’s input slot and adds the values together:

sentences.en: The private computation is written in StoffelLang.
sentences.zh: 私有计算是用 StoffelLang 编写的。

sentences.en: Values marked secret remain shared while the program operates on them.
sentences.zh: 当程序对其进行操作时，标记为秘密的值仍然是共享的。

sentences.en: The core aggregation function reads one private value from each user’s input slot and adds the values together:
sentences.zh: 核心聚合函数从每个用户的输入槽中读取一个私有值并将这些值加在一起：

blocks.html_en: The program repeats this for all six SNPs. It reveals only the cohort totals and then calculates the weighted cohort score. It never deliberately reveals an individual contribution.

sentences.en: The program repeats this for all six SNPs.
sentences.zh: 该程序对所有六个 SNP 重复此操作。

sentences.en: It reveals only the cohort totals and then calculates the weighted cohort score.
sentences.zh: 它仅显示队列总数，然后计算加权队列分数。

sentences.en: It never deliberately reveals an individual contribution.
sentences.zh: 它从不刻意揭示个人的贡献。

blocks.html_en: The four parties use Stoffel’s HoneyBadger backend over the BLS12-381 scalar field. In developer terms, HoneyBadger is Stoffel’s general-purpose engine for private arithmetic. The configuration uses four parties and a threshold of one, satisfying HoneyBadger’s n >= 3t + 1 requirement and tolerating one faulty party under the protocol’s assumptions so one bad apple cannot ruin the basket of MPC nodes.

sentences.en: The four parties use Stoffel’s HoneyBadger backend over the BLS12-381 scalar field.
sentences.zh: 四方在 BLS12-381 标量字段上使用 Stoffel 的 HoneyBadger 后端。

sentences.en: In developer terms, HoneyBadger is Stoffel’s general-purpose engine for private arithmetic.
sentences.zh: 从开发者的角度来说，HoneyBadger 是 Stoffel 的私有算术通用引擎。

sentences.en: The configuration uses four parties and a threshold of one, satisfying HoneyBadger’s n >= 3t + 1 requirement and tolerating one faulty party under the protocol’s assumptions so one bad apple cannot ruin the basket of MPC nodes.
sentences.zh: 该配置使用四个参与方和一个阈值，满足 HoneyBadger 的 n >= 3t + 1 要求，并在协议的假设下容忍一个错误的参与方，这样一个坏苹果就不会破坏一篮子 MPC 节点。

blocks.html_en: The runner provisions identities, compiles the program, starts the infrastructure and launches clients 50 milliseconds apart. It independently calculates the expected result for the deterministic synthetic fixture, checks the aggregate emitted by the MPC parties and records timing and memory usage. Everything is torn down after the run.

sentences.en: The runner provisions identities, compiles the program, starts the infrastructure and launches clients 50 milliseconds apart.
sentences.zh: 运行者提供身份、编译程序、启动基础设施并以 50 毫秒的间隔启动客户端。

sentences.en: It independently calculates the expected result for the deterministic synthetic fixture, checks the aggregate emitted by the MPC parties and records timing and memory usage.
sentences.zh: 它独立计算确定性合成装置的预期结果，检查 MPC 各方发出的聚合并记录时间和内存使用情况。

sentences.en: Everything is torn down after the run.
sentences.zh: 跑步后一切都被拆除了。

sentences.en: On my development PC, the benchmark measured:

sentences.en: approximately 1.29 GiB peak memory across the one hundred client processes; and
sentences.zh: 一百个客户端进程的峰值内存约为 1.29 GiB；和

blocks.html_en: • approximately 62 MiB peak memory across the coordinator and four MPC containers.

sentences.en: approximately 62 MiB peak memory across the coordinator and four MPC containers.

blocks.html_en: Most of the elapsed time was infrastructure startup rather than the private calculation. This is a local proof of concept rather than a production benchmark but an end-to-end run in under a minute was encouraging.

sentences.en: Most of the elapsed time was infrastructure startup rather than the private calculation.
sentences.zh: 大部分时间都花在了基础设施启动上，而不是私人计算上。

sentences.en: This is a local proof of concept rather than a production benchmark but an end-to-end run in under a minute was encouraging.
sentences.zh: 这是本地概念验证，而不是生产基准，但一分钟内的端到端运行令人鼓舞。

blocks.html_en: Scaling from ten to one hundred clients also produced a useful infrastructure lesson. Every client connects to the coordinator and all four parties, creating roughly five hundred client-to-server connections. Launching all clients simultaneously caused intermittent TLS failures while clients waited for MPC input masks. The cryptographic computation was not the bottleneck; the issue was the initial burst of network connections.

sentences.en: Scaling from ten to one hundred clients also produced a useful infrastructure lesson.
sentences.zh: 从十个客户端扩展到一百个客户端也产生了有用的基础设施课程。

sentences.en: Every client connects to the coordinator and all four parties, creating roughly five hundred client-to-server connections.
sentences.zh: 每个客户端都连接到协调器和所有四方，创建大约 500 个客户端到服务器的连接。

sentences.en: Launching all clients simultaneously caused intermittent TLS failures while clients waited for MPC input masks.
sentences.zh: 在客户端等待 MPC 输入掩码时，同时启动所有客户端会导致间歇性 TLS 失败。

sentences.en: The cryptographic computation was not the bottleneck; the issue was the initial burst of network connections.
sentences.zh: 密码计算不是瓶颈；问题是网络连接的最初爆发。

blocks.html_en: Staggering client launches by just 50 milliseconds spread the handshake burst over about five seconds and produced a successful run. It was an encouragingly small adjustment, and a useful reminder that an MPC application is more than its cryptographic protocol. Identity provisioning, TLS handshakes, connection limits, retries, backoff and observability all matter as participation grows.

sentences.en: Staggering client launches by just 50 milliseconds spread the handshake burst over about five seconds and produced a successful run.
sentences.zh: 客户端启动时间仅相差 50 毫秒，握手突发时间延长了约 5 秒，并成功运行。

sentences.en: It was an encouragingly small adjustment, and a useful reminder that an MPC application is more than its cryptographic protocol.
sentences.zh: 这是一个令人鼓舞的小调整，也是一个有用的提醒：MPC 应用程序不仅仅是其加密协议。

sentences.en: Identity provisioning, TLS handshakes, connection limits, retries, backoff and observability all matter as participation grows.
sentences.zh: 随着参与度的增长，身份配置、TLS 握手、连接限制、重试、退避和可观察性都很重要。

blocks.html_en: Developer experience

sentences.en: Developer experience
sentences.zh: 开发者经验

blocks.html_en: So does Stoffel make MPC-ing easy? The core developer experience was one of the most encouraging parts of the experiment. StoffelLang is small and readable, its secret types make the privacy boundary visible in the program, and writing the six private sums felt much closer to ordinary application development than to implementing a cryptographic protocol. The repository examples provided a useful path to getting a local coordinator and MPC parties running.

sentences.en: So does Stoffel make MPC-ing easy?
sentences.zh: 那么 Stoffel 会让 MPC 变得容易吗？

sentences.en: The core developer experience was one of the most encouraging parts of the experiment.
sentences.zh: 核心开发人员体验是实验中最令人鼓舞的部分之一。

sentences.en: StoffelLang is small and readable, its secret types make the privacy boundary visible in the program, and writing the six private sums felt much closer to ordinary application development than to implementing a cryptographic protocol.
sentences.zh: StoffelLang 体积小且可读，其秘密类型使隐私边界在程序中可见，编写六个私有和感觉更接近普通应用程序开发，而不是实现加密协议。

sentences.en: The repository examples provided a useful path to getting a local coordinator and MPC parties running.
sentences.zh: 存储库示例提供了使本地协调器和 MPC 方运行的有用路径。

blocks.html_en: For a version 0.1.0 release, the documentation and examples gave me enough to move from a small example to a working 100-client experiment. The straightforward single-client path is explained clearly. This PoC went beyond that path, using many separately identified clients outside Docker and tuning input-mask capacity and output authorization, so some answers came from the examples and source code rather than a single guide. The code was readable enough to make that investigation productive.

sentences.en: For a version 0.1.0 release, the documentation and examples gave me enough to move from a small example to a working 100-client experiment.
sentences.zh: 对于 0.1.0 版本，文档和示例足以让我从一个小示例转向可运行的 100 个客户端实验。

sentences.en: The straightforward single-client path is explained clearly.
sentences.zh: 简单的单客户端路径得到了清晰的解释。

sentences.en: This PoC went beyond that path, using many separately identified clients outside Docker and tuning input-mask capacity and output authorization, so some answers came from the examples and source code rather than a single guide.
sentences.zh: 这个 PoC 超越了这条路径，使用了 Docker 之外的许多单独识别的客户端，并调整了输入掩码容量和输出授权，因此一些答案来自示例和源代码，而不是单个指南。

sentences.en: The code was readable enough to make that investigation productive.
sentences.zh: 该代码的可读性足以使调查富有成效。

blocks.html_en: The fact that the private computation was the easiest part is a good sign for Stoffel’s developer-platform goal. As the platform matures, end-to-end deployment guides, a configuration reference, troubleshooting documentation and supported client SDKs could make the same experience even smoother for developers who do not want to inspect implementation details.

sentences.en: The fact that the private computation was the easiest part is a good sign for Stoffel’s developer-platform goal.
sentences.zh: 事实上，私有计算是最简单的部分，这对于 Stoffel 的开发者平台目标来说是一个好兆头。

sentences.en: As the platform matures, end-to-end deployment guides, a configuration reference, troubleshooting documentation and supported client SDKs could make the same experience even smoother for developers who do not want to inspect implementation details.

blocks.html_en: The test also clarified the next steps from a successful PoC toward production:

blocks.html_en: • All four MPC parties ran on one PC. The protocol separated their data, but one machine is not four independent organizations. A production deployment needs parties operated by entities that are unlikely to collude. With this configuration, privacy assumes no more than one party is faulty or colluding. Happily, the Stoffel team provided me with a API key to their MPC cluster so I can test a near-production deployment soon.

Production still needs minimum cohort sizes, query controls, consent, auditing and a separate identity authorized to receive results.

blocks.html_en: Conclusion

sentences.en: Conclusion
sentences.zh: 结论

blocks.html_en: Stoffel made the private computation itself pleasantly straightforward. StoffelLang was expressive enough for the aggregate, the four-party network produced the correct result and one hundred independently identified clients completed the study on a single development machine.

Stoffel allowed the experiment to move the trust boundary away from a central genotype database and into explicit product and infrastructure decisions that can be designed and audited.

blocks.html_en: There is naturally more work between a version 0.1.0 PoC and a production deployment. The immediate next step is to run the program on a real cluster operated by the Stoffel team, moving beyond the single-machine local setup (coming soon!). Beyond that, useful steps include independently operated parties, audited mobile client code, resilient connection handling and larger load tests. Still, Stoffel successfully demonstrated the most important part: a credible path to genomic studies that do not begin by collecting everyone’s raw DNA in one database.

content_html_zh:
大多数基因组研究首先要求参与者上传他们拥有的最具识别性和不可撤销的数据之一，即他们的 DNA。 我继续研究多方计算是否可以提供不同的模型，在该模型中，研究可以产生有用的结果，而无需任何人首先收集参与者的基因组。

这个问题现在促使我使用 Stoffel MPC 构建概念验证。 一百名模拟参与者成功计算了等位基因总数，而没有任何一方看到完整的数据集。 这篇文章解释了我构建了什么、它如何执行以及我学到了什么。

Stoffel MPC 已推出

Stoffel 最近推出了其多方计算 (MPC) 平台的 0.1.0 版本，其雄心勃勃的目标是让普通开发人员而不仅仅是密码学研究团队可以访问隐私保护应用程序。

MPC 允许多台计算机对私有数据进行计算，而无需任何一台计算机接收完整的输入。 尽管传统上很难使用 Stoffel 构建 MPC 系统，但该技术仍是一个平易近人的开发平台。 应用程序是用类似 Python 的 StoffelLang 编写的，编译为字节码，并由 Stoffel VM 在一组 MPC 方上执行。

我们不再需要依赖更强大的隐私政策或更安全的中央数据库。 我们可以简单地避免在一处收集原始数据。

当然，我想尝试一下 Monadic DNA

鉴于我正在进行的 Monadic DNA 工作，基因组学是一个明显的测试案例。 在早期的实验中，我和我的同事使用了来自三十名参与者的十亿和真实基因型数据来探索私人 DNA 分析。

基因数据是对隐私系统的一个非常好的测试。 它具有高度识别性，可以泄露有关亲属的信息，并且在泄露后无法更改。 与此同时，许多有用的基因组研究不需要检查个体记录。 研究人员可能只需要队列水平的变异计数或整个组计算的分数。

我想要探索的具体用例是改进的 Monadic DNA 移动应用程序。 Monadic DNA 用户将他们的基因型数据保存在手机上，并可能选择参与一项需要对许多用户进行汇总统计的研究（或许作为付费的回报）。 问题是，该应用程序是否可以安全地贡献这些数据，而无需首先将用户的完整基因型上传到 Monadic DNA、研究人员或其他值得信赖的中央服务。

我想看看 Stoffel 是否可以让每个 Monadic DNA 应用程序直接参与联合计算，而不是采用将每个人的基因型数据上传到必须保护它的中央服务的传统设计。 在该设计中，协调器、应用程序服务器或单个 MPC 方不会接收完整的数据集。

该实验的源代码可在 github.com/vishakh/stoffel-test 获取。

概念验证的目的是什么

概念验证模拟了一百名 Monadic DNA 移动应用用户参与基因组聚合研究。 每个用户贡献六个合成 SNP 值，他们可以通过 Monadic DNA、23andMe 或其他服务获得这些值。 该研究计算了每个目标等位基因的总数以及整个队列的简单加权得分。

重要的部分是信任边界。 每个模拟用户都有一个单独的客户端身份和一个仅包含该用户的六个值的进程。 客户端在本地创建共享并将不同的共享直接发送到每个 MPC 方。 仅显示总计数和分数。

这与将所有一百条记录放入一个服务器端客户端是故意不同的。 这将展示 MPC 网络内部的私有算术，但客户端仍将是一个可信的数据收集器，能够查看或泄露每个基因型。

在预期的 Monadic DNA 流程中，用户将在移动应用程序中查看并同意一项研究。 该应用程序将仅选择经批准的计算所需的 DNA 值，在手机上创建共享并将其直接发送给 MPC 各方。 完整的 DNA 数据不应通过 Monadic DNA 的服务器或其他中央摄取服务；只有批准的汇总结果才应离开 MPC 计算。

MPC 和全同态加密的比较

多方计算 (MPC) 和完全同态加密 (FHE) 都可以在不暴露底层输入的情况下计算数据，但它们使用不同的模型。 使用 FHE，每个用户通常使用公钥对其基因型进行加密，服务器直接对密文进行计算。 通过 MPC，每个用户将其输入分成多个份额，并将不同的份额发送给多个计算方中的每一个。

这些方法将信任和操作复杂性放在不同的地方。 FHE 系统需要制定谁可以解密结果的策略。 这可能涉及一个密钥持有者或多方共享的阈值密钥。 MPC 不依赖于一个解密密钥持有者，但它需要多个足够独立的参与方进行通信并在计算过程中保持可用。

性能特征还因工作负载和实施而异。 FHE 支持单个服务的非交互式加密输入和计算，尽管密文操作的计算成本可能很高。 MPC 引入了各方之间的通信，而简单的聚合算术（例如本实验中的求和）是自然的 MPC 工作负载。 这两种方法对于基因组学来说都不是更好。 适当的选择取决于计算、信任假设、部署约束和所需的交互模型。 此 PoC 通过 Stoffel 探索 MPC 选项，而不是试图建立相对于 FHE 的总体优势。

要了解有关使用 FHE 构建消费者应用程序的更多信息，请参阅 https://vishakh.blog/2025/08/06/lessons-from-using-fhe-to-build-a-secure-consumer-app/。

2026 年 8 月，我还将在 HOPE 26 会议上发表有关 FHE 的演讲：https://schedule.hope.net/hope26/talks#Using-Fully-Homomorphic-Encryption-to-Build-Real-World-Software

技术细节

本地设置具有三种类型的组件：

  • 一百个本机客户端进程代表独立用户或移动设备。 每个都有一个独特的、短暂的身份，并且只有自己的六个合成 SNP 值。
  • 四个 Stoffel MPC 参与方接收每个客户的份额并共同执行私密计算。
  • 协调员授权参与者并处理发现。 它不接收基因型值或执行基因组计算。

协调者和四个 MPC 方在 Docker 中运行。 客户端作为独立的本机进程在 Docker 网络外部运行，并通过公开的本地地址进行连接。 这表明用户端客户端本质上不需要与 MPC 基础设施一起运行。

私有计算是用 StoffelLang 编写的。 当程序对其进行操作时，标记为秘密的值仍然是共享的。 核心聚合函数从每个用户的输入槽中读取一个私有值并将这些值加在一起：

该程序对所有六个 SNP 重复此操作。 它仅显示队列总数，然后计算加权队列分数。 它从不刻意揭示个人的贡献。

四方在 BLS12-381 标量字段上使用 Stoffel 的 HoneyBadger 后端。 从开发者的角度来说，HoneyBadger 是 Stoffel 的私有算术通用引擎。 该配置使用四个参与方和一个阈值，满足 HoneyBadger 的 n >= 3t + 1 要求，并在协议的假设下容忍一个错误的参与方，这样一个坏苹果就不会破坏一篮子 MPC 节点。

运行者提供身份、编译程序、启动基础设施并以 50 毫秒的间隔启动客户端。 它独立计算确定性合成装置的预期结果，检查 MPC 各方发出的聚合并记录时间和内存使用情况。 跑步后一切都被拆除了。

发现

一百个用户的运行成功完成并产生了预期值：

加权队列得分为 498，与赛程独立计算的结果相符。

在我的开发 PC 上，基准测试测量为：

  • 28.26 秒启动并准备基础设施；
  • 6.17 秒启动客户端并完成 MPC 工作负载；
  • 完整运行时间为 45.29 秒，包括设置、验证和清理；
  • 一百个客户端进程的峰值内存约为 1.29 GiB；和
  • 协调器和四个 MPC 容器的峰值内存约为 62 MiB。

大部分时间都花在了基础设施启动上，而不是私人计算上。 这是本地概念验证，而不是生产基准，但一分钟内的端到端运行令人鼓舞。

从十个客户端扩展到一百个客户端也产生了有用的基础设施课程。 每个客户端都连接到协调器和所有四方，创建大约 500 个客户端到服务器的连接。 在客户端等待 MPC 输入掩码时，同时启动所有客户端会导致间歇性 TLS 失败。 密码计算不是瓶颈；问题是网络连接的最初爆发。

客户端启动时间仅相差 50 毫秒，握手突发时间延长了约 5 秒，并成功运行。 这是一个令人鼓舞的小调整，也是一个有用的提醒：MPC 应用程序不仅仅是其加密协议。 随着参与度的增长，身份配置、TLS 握手、连接限制、重试、退避和可观察性都很重要。

开发者经验

那么 Stoffel 会让 MPC 变得容易吗？ 核心开发人员体验是实验中最令人鼓舞的部分之一。 StoffelLang 体积小且可读，其秘密类型使隐私边界在程序中可见，编写六个私有和感觉更接近普通应用程序开发，而不是实现加密协议。 存储库示例提供了使本地协调器和 MPC 方运行的有用路径。

对于 0.1.0 版本，文档和示例足以让我从一个小示例转向可运行的 100 个客户端实验。 简单的单客户端路径得到了清晰的解释。 这个 PoC 超越了这条路径，使用了 Docker 之外的许多单独识别的客户端，并调整了输入掩码容量和输出授权，因此一些答案来自示例和源代码，而不是单个指南。 该代码的可读性足以使调查富有成效。

事实上，私有计算是最简单的部分，这对于 Stoffel 的开发者平台目标来说是一个好兆头。 随着平台的成熟，端到端部署指南、配置参考、故障排除文档和支持的客户端 SDK 可以让不想检查实施细节的开发人员获得相同的体验。

该测试还阐明了从成功的 PoC 到生产的后续步骤：

  • 所有四个 MPC 方都在一台 PC 上运行。 协议将他们的数据分开，但一台机器并不是四个独立的组织。 生产部署需要由不太可能串通的实体运营的各方。 通过这种配置，隐私假设只有一方有过错或共谋。 令人高兴的是，Stoffel 团队为我提供了他们的 MPC 集群的 API 密钥，这样我就可以很快测试接近生产的部署。
  • 输入是确定性的和综合的。 测试工具知道它们，因此可以验证答案。 在实际研究中，独立运行的设备必须在本地生成共享。
  • MPC 保护计算，而不是结果的安全。 小群体、重复查询或重叠组可以通过减法揭示个人贡献。 生产仍然需要最小队列规模、查询控制、同意、审核和授权接收结果的单独身份。
  • 该存储库尚未提供可用于生产的移动 SDK。 原生 Rust 客户端代表手机；打包和审核 iOS 和 Android 的逻辑仍然是必要的。 使用数百种移动设备的 MPC 仍然相当小众，因此缺乏移动 SDK 并非不合理。
  • 成功的发布令人震惊非常适合这个 PoC。 测试 1000 个并发客户端将是下一个规模里程碑，并且需要更高的 RPC 限制、强大的重试、更多的内存和现实的基础设施。

结论

斯托菲尔使私人计算本身变得非常简单。 StoffelLang 的总体表现力足够强，四方网络产生了正确的结果，一百个独立识别的客户在一台开发机器上完成了研究。

剩下的问题围绕着计算，即谁在操作各方、如何发布身份、移动客户端断开连接时会发生什么以及哪些聚合可以安全地泄露。 斯托菲尔允许实验将信任边界从中央基因型数据库转移到可以设计和审核的明确产品和基础设施决策中。

0.1.0 PoC 版本和生产部署之间自然还有更多工作要做。 下一步是在 Stoffel 团队运营的真实集群上运行该程序，超越单机本地设置（即将推出！）。 除此之外，有用的步骤包括独立运营方、经过审核的移动客户端代码、弹性连接处理和更大的负载测试。 尽管如此，Stoffel 仍然成功地展示了最重要的部分：一条可靠的基因组研究路径，并不是从将每个人的原始 DNA 收集到一个数据库中开始的。

对于初始版本和本地概念验证来说，这是一个令人印象深刻的结果。 这让我对 Stoffel 持谨慎乐观态度，并真正有兴趣随着平台的发展进一步利用它进行建设。
